name: Farmhouse Kitchen
paraphrase: Make the kitchen big enough to be a room in its own right, with a large central table where the household actually eats and gathers, rather than a narrow galley reserved only for cooking. Alexander observes that the modern split between an efficient work-kitchen and a separate dining room destroys the convivial rhythm of preparing and sharing a meal together, so cooking, eating and informal sitting should all happen in one warm, shared space arranged around the table.
